_provenance: Claude-authored original passages (DeepSeek not used). R13 batch4-society. CET-4 选词填空 style: ~250-280-word original passage, 10 numbered blanks (1)____ .. (10)____, 15-word bank (10 answers + 5 distractors), answers = distinct bank indices 0-14, each blank grammatically + semantically unique-fit; 5 distractors fit no blank.

sets.passage: Every Saturday morning, a small group of students gathers at the edge of our town to do something many of their classmates find strange: they spend hours cleaning the riverbank for no pay at all. Their (1)____ is simple. They believe that a healthy community depends on ordinary people who are willing to (2)____ their free time for others. Volunteering, they argue, is not a (3)____ act but a shared responsibility. At first, only a few people joined. The work was (4)____ and the results were hard to see. Yet the volunteers kept coming, and slowly the river grew cleaner. Local shops began to (5)____ them with free drinks, and neighbours who had once ignored the project started to (6)____ a few hours of their weekends to the cause. What had seemed like a hopeless task became a source of real (7)____. The students soon noticed something unexpected. By serving others, they had gained (8)____ skills that no classroom could teach. They learned how to (9)____ disagreements, plan events, and speak in public with confidence. Most importantly, they felt that their efforts (10)____. Today the riverbank project has spread to three nearby towns, proving that community service can change far more than a single stretch of water. Parents now bring their children along on weekends, and retired engineers offer advice on cleaning the muddy banks more safely. Local newspapers have begun to report on the steady progress, and a few schools have asked the students to give short talks about how they first began. It reminds us that genuine kindness, once started, has a quiet power to grow and to draw strangers together into something that finally feels like a true neighbourhood.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 motive,1 sacrifice,2 selfish,3 exhausting,4 reward,5 contribute,6 pride,7 valuable,8 resolve,9 mattered,10 ignore,11 salary,12 rapidly,13 purchase,14 decorate。(1) Their motive is simple 动机=0；(2) willing to sacrifice their free time 牺牲(动词原形)=1；(3) not a selfish act 自私的(形容词修饰 act)=2；(4) work was exhausting 累人的(形容词作表语)=3；(5) reward them with free drinks 用…回报(reward sb with)=4；(6) contribute a few hours … to the cause 贡献…给(某事业)(动词原形，contribute sth to 固定搭配，与 sacrifice 区分：sacrifice 不接 to the cause)=5；(7) source of real pride 自豪感(名词)=6；(8) valuable skills 有价值的(形容词修饰 skills)=7；(9) resolve disagreements 化解分歧(动词原形)=8；(10) efforts mattered 重要/有意义(过去式不及物)=9。干扰词: ignore(语义相反，不可'忽视他人时间'), salary(名词且与'no pay'矛盾), rapidly(副词无槽位), purchase(语义为购买不合空5/6), decorate(装饰，语义不合)。

sets.passage: Across much of the world, populations are growing older at a speed that few governments had expected. As birth rates fall and people live longer, the (1)____ of citizens above sixty is rising fast. This change brings serious challenges. Pension systems were designed for an age when many young workers (2)____ a small number of retired people, but that balance is now breaking down. Hospitals face (3)____ demand for long-term care, and the cost of treatment continues to climb. Yet an aging society is not only a (4)____. Older people often possess deep experience and patience that the young have not yet had time to (5)____. Many remain active far beyond the traditional retirement age, choosing to (6)____ part-time or to mentor newcomers in their former trade. Smart companies have begun to (7)____ this resource rather than waste it. Governments, meanwhile, are searching for ways to keep the elderly (8)____ and connected. Some build community centres where seniors can take classes; others (9)____ technology that lets them live safely at home. The goal is to ensure that a longer life is also a (10)____ one. Families, too, are rethinking how they share the duty of caring for elderly relatives, since the burden can no longer rest on one daughter or son alone. Volunteers visit lonely seniors, neighbours run errands for those who cannot drive, and local clubs organise regular outings so that nobody feels forgotten. How a nation treats its oldest members reveals a great deal about its values. An aging society, handled wisely, need not mean decline; it can instead become a chance to redefine what a full and meaningful life truly means.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 proportion,1 supported,2 rising,3 burden,4 acquire,5 work,6 value,7 independent,8 promote,9 fulfilling,10 shrink,11 approximate,12 obvious,13 purchase,14 ancient。(1) the proportion of citizens 比例(名词)=0；(2) many young workers supported a small number 供养(过去式及物)=1；(3) rising demand 不断上升的(现在分词作定语)=2；(4) not only a burden 负担(名词)=3；(5) had not yet had time to acquire 获得(动词原形)=4；(6) choosing to work part-time 工作(动词原形)=5；(7) value this resource 重视(动词原形)=6；(8) keep the elderly independent 独立的(形容词作宾补)=7；(9) promote technology 推广(动词原形)=8；(10) a fulfilling one 充实的(形容词修饰 one=life)=9。干扰词: shrink(收缩，与'rising'相反语义不合), approximate(近似的，无合适槽), obvious(明显的，不合空4 burden), purchase(购买，不合), ancient(古代的，不合)。

sets.passage: When an old temple or a row of historic houses is torn down to make space for a shopping mall, something more than bricks is lost. Each building carries the memory of the people who once lived and worked there, and once it is gone that memory can never be fully (1)____. This is why the effort to (2)____ cultural heritage matters so much. Heritage is not merely a collection of beautiful objects in a glass case. It is a living link to our (3)____, a thread that connects one generation to the next. A traditional craft, a local dialect, or an ancient festival can be just as (4)____ as a famous monument. Sadly, such treasures are often (5)____ in the rush toward modern life. Young people move to the cities, old skills are forgotten, and few apprentices remain to (6)____ the work of their teachers. Protecting heritage therefore requires more than money; it demands a (7)____ effort by ordinary citizens. Schools can teach children to (8)____ their local history. Craftsmen can be paid a fair wage so that their knowledge does not (9)____ with them. Governments can pass laws that prevent the (10)____ of important sites. Museums and libraries can record fading songs and stories before the last people who remember them are gone. Even simple choices matter: families who cook old recipes or speak their grandparents' language at home are quietly keeping a culture alive. None of this means freezing a culture in the past. On the contrary, a society that honours its roots is far better prepared to grow with both confidence and pride into an uncertain future.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 restored,1 preserve,2 past,3 precious,4 neglected,5 continue,6 collective,7 appreciate,8 vanish,9 destruction,10 increase,11 modern,12 rapidly,13 purchase,14 lonely。(1) never be fully restored 修复/恢复(被动过去分词)=0；(2) effort to preserve cultural heritage 保护(动词原形)=1；(3) link to our past 过去(名词)=2；(4) just as precious as 珍贵的(形容词)=3；(5) often neglected in the rush 被忽视(被动过去分词)=4；(6) remain to continue the work 延续(动词原形)=5；(7) a collective effort 集体的(形容词修饰 effort)=6；(8) teach children to appreciate 欣赏/珍视(动词原形)=7；(9) does not vanish with them 消失(动词原形)=8；(10) prevent the destruction of sites 破坏(名词)=9。干扰词: increase(增加，与'prevent the …'语义不合且空10需名词破坏义), modern(现代的，不合空3), rapidly(副词无槽), purchase(购买，不合), lonely(孤独的，不合)。

sets.passage: In the past fifty years, cities around the globe have expanded at an astonishing rate, drawing millions of people from the countryside in search of work. This rapid (1)____ has reshaped the planet. Where there were once fields and villages, towers of glass and concrete now (2)____ into the sky. For many migrants, the city offers a real (3)____ to escape poverty and to give their children a better education. Yet such fast growth comes at a heavy (4)____. Roads become hopelessly crowded, the air grows (5)____, and affordable housing is increasingly hard to find. Newcomers may earn more than before, but they often feel (6)____ from the close ties they left behind in their home towns. Planners now realise that a city cannot simply keep (7)____ outward forever. Instead, they must design neighbourhoods that are dense yet (8)____, with parks, schools, and shops within easy walking distance. Good public transport can greatly (9)____ the number of cars on the road, while green spaces help to cool the streets and lift people's spirits. The challenge is to make growth (10)____ rather than chaotic. Some governments have begun to invest heavily in new subway lines and bus routes, hoping to ease the daily crush of commuters. Others encourage builders to mix flats, offices, and shops within the same block, so that fewer people must travel long distances each morning. Urbanization, after all, is not going to stop, and few would truly wish it to. The real question is whether tomorrow's cities will be crowded traps that exhaust their residents, or carefully planned communities where millions of strangers can genuinely live well together.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 expansion,1 rise,2 chance,3 price,4 polluted,5 isolated,6 spreading,7 livable,8 reduce,9 sustainable,10 decline,11 ancient,12 rapidly,13 purchase,14 cheerful。(1) This rapid expansion 扩张(名词)=0；(2) towers rise into the sky 耸立(动词原形，主语 towers 复数)=1；(3) a real chance to escape 机会(名词)=2；(4) at a heavy price 代价(名词)=3；(5) the air grows polluted 被污染的(形容词作表语)=4；(6) feel isolated from 孤立的(形容词)=5；(7) keep spreading outward 蔓延(现在分词，keep doing)=6；(8) dense yet livable 宜居的(形容词)=7；(9) reduce the number of cars 减少(动词原形)=8；(10) make growth sustainable 可持续的(形容词作宾补)=9。干扰词: decline(衰退，与'expansion/growth'矛盾), ancient(古代的，不合), rapidly(副词，空均不收副词), purchase(购买，不合), cheerful(快乐的，不合空7宜居义)。

sets.passage: Ask people why museums still matter in the age of the internet, and you will hear many different answers. Some say a museum is simply a place to (1)____ rare and beautiful objects. But its true role is far richer than that. A good museum does not just store the past; it brings history to (2)____. Standing before a real ancient tool or a faded letter, a visitor feels a connection that no photograph on a screen can fully (3)____. Museums are also powerful tools for (4)____. School groups arrive every week, and children who are bored by textbooks suddenly become (5)____ when they can see and almost touch the evidence of earlier lives. In this way the museum quietly (6)____ a love of learning. Modern museums have worked hard to throw off their old, dusty (7)____. Many now use interactive screens, sound, and light to make exhibits come (8)____. Some invite visitors to handle replicas or to take part in experiments. Such changes have helped to (9)____ a far wider audience, including those who once thought museums were dull. Many museums now stay open late, host evening concerts, and design special trails for visitors who are blind or who cannot easily walk. By opening their doors in these thoughtful ways, they prove that they belong to everyone, not only to scholars or wealthy collectors. Of course, no amount of clever technology can ever fully replace the (10)____ of standing in a quiet hall before a genuine masterpiece. That silent meeting between a person and an object across the centuries remains one of the simplest yet most moving experiences a city can offer its citizens.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 display,1 life,2 capture,3 education,4 curious,5 encourages,6 image,7 alive,8 attract,9 wonder,10 ignore,11 ancient,12 rapidly,13 purchase,14 boring。(1) a place to display objects 展示(动词原形)=0；(2) brings history to life (bring sth to life 固定搭配)=1；(3) no photograph can fully capture 捕捉/再现(动词原形)=2；(4) tools for education 教育(名词)=3；(5) become curious 好奇的(形容词作表语)=4；(6) the museum quietly encourages 培养/鼓励(第三人称单数及物动词，主语 museum)=5；(7) old, dusty image 形象(名词)=6；(8) make exhibits come alive (come alive 固定搭配)=7；(9) attract a far wider audience 吸引(动词原形)=8；(10) the wonder of standing 惊叹/奇妙(名词)=9。干扰词: ignore(忽视，语义相反), ancient(古代的，文中已另有 ancient 不需), rapidly(副词无槽), purchase(购买，不合), boring(无聊的，与空5 curious 矛盾)。

sets.passage: In every country there are festivals whose roots stretch back hundreds of years. They mark the changing seasons, honour the dead, or celebrate a harvest long since gathered. For our grandparents, these days followed a fixed (1)____, full of careful rituals that everyone understood. Today, however, the place of traditional festivals in modern life is far from (2)____. Busy schedules, long working hours, and the pull of the smartphone have all begun to (3)____ the old customs. Some young people complain that the ceremonies feel (4)____ and have little to do with their daily lives. Others worry that festivals are slowly being turned into nothing more than a chance to (5)____ goods, stripped of their deeper meaning. Yet it would be a mistake to (6)____ these celebrations off too quickly. A well-kept festival can give a scattered family a rare reason to (7)____ under one roof. It offers a shared rhythm that the rushing modern world badly (8)____. The wisest approach may be to let traditions gently (9)____ rather than freeze them. A festival can keep its core spirit while welcoming new foods, new music, and new faces. Communities around the world have already shown how this can work. In some towns, young volunteers now teach old dances on video, while elderly cooks share recipes that might otherwise have been forgotten forever. Families travel long distances to gather, lanterns are lit, and children hear the same stories their great-grandparents once told. When that happens, an ancient celebration does not merely survive; it slowly becomes a living (10)____ between who we once were and who we still hope one day to become.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 pattern,1 secure,2 weaken,3 meaningless,4 sell,5 write,6 reunite,7 lacks,8 evolve,9 bridge,10 ignore,11 ancient,12 rapidly,13 gravity,14 modern。(1) followed a fixed pattern 模式(名词)=0；(2) far from secure 稳固的(形容词)=1；(3) begun to weaken the old customs 削弱(动词原形)=2；(4) ceremonies feel meaningless 无意义的(形容词作表语)=3；(5) a chance to sell goods 出售(动词原形)=4；(6) to write these celebrations off (write off 固定搭配 否定/勾销)=5；(7) a reason to reunite under one roof 团聚(动词原形)=6；(8) the modern world badly lacks 缺乏(第三人称单数及物，主语 world)=7；(9) let traditions gently evolve 演变(动词原形)=8；(10) a living bridge between 桥梁(名词)=9。干扰词: ignore(忽视，不合 write…off 搭配), ancient(古代的，文中已有 ancient), rapidly(副词无槽), gravity(重力/严肃，名词，空1需 pattern、空10需 bridge，均无'重力'义，无合适槽；惰性干扰), modern(现代的，已出现，无合适槽)。

sets.passage: It is easy to blame the young and the old for failing to understand each other. Teenagers roll their eyes at advice they find old-fashioned, while their elders complain that nothing about modern habits makes any (1)____. This so-called generation gap is real, but it is not as wide as it seems, and there are practical ways to (2)____ it until it almost disappears. The first step is to recognise that each side has something the other (3)____. Older people carry the wisdom of long (4)____, including hard lessons about money, work, and loss. The young, in turn, bring fresh energy and an easy (5)____ with new technology. Problems arise mainly when neither side bothers to (6)____. A grandparent who refuses to learn how to send a message, and a grandchild who is too impatient to explain it, will quickly grow (7)____ from each other. The solution is surprisingly ordinary: spend time together on a shared task. Cooking a family meal, repairing a bicycle, or simply telling stories can (8)____ the distance between ages, connecting one side to the other. Schools and communities can help by creating projects in which the two generations (9)____ as equals. When that happens, respect tends to flow in both directions. Patience helps enormously on both sides. An older person who slows down to explain the reason behind a rule, and a younger one who asks honest questions instead of mocking, will usually discover that they share far more than they expected. The gap, it turns out, is rarely caused by genuine dislike. More often it is simply the result of two busy groups of people who have never once been given a real (10)____ to sit down and listen to one another.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 sense,1 narrow,2 lacks,3 experience,4 comfort,5 listen,6 distant,7 bridge,8 cooperate,9 chance,10 ignore,11 ancient,12 rapidly,13 purchase,14 modern。(1) makes any sense (make sense 固定搭配)=0；(2) ways to narrow it until it almost disappears 缩小直至几乎消失(动词原形，宾语 it=gap；'缩小直至消失'只配 narrow 缩小义，bridge 架桥不能使 gap 消失，借此与 bridge 区分)=1；(3) something the other lacks 缺乏(第三人称单数及物，主语 side)=2；(4) wisdom of long experience 经验(名词)=3；(5) an easy comfort with new technology 自在/熟练(名词，comfort with)=4；(6) bothers to listen 倾听(动词原形)=5；(7) grow distant from 疏远的(形容词作表语)=6；(8) bridge the distance … connecting one side to the other (bridge 作动词 跨接/弥合，需'连接两端'义，narrow 缩小不含连接义，借此与 narrow 区分)=7；(9) the two generations cooperate as equals 合作(动词原形，主语复数)=8；(10) a real chance to listen 机会(名词)=9。干扰词: ignore(忽视，空6需 listen 倾听义), ancient(古代的，不合), rapidly(副词无槽), purchase(购买，不合), modern(现代的，文中已有 modern habits/technology，无空)。

sets.passage: When we think about what makes a city work, we tend to picture its tallest towers or its famous landmarks. Yet the real backbone of any great city is something far less glamorous: its public transport. Buses, trains, and subways quietly (1)____ millions of people every day, carrying them to work, to school, and back home again. The value of such a system goes well beyond simple (2)____. A reliable network gives freedom to those who cannot afford a car, allowing the young, the poor, and the elderly to remain (3)____ in city life. It also helps to (4)____ the terrible traffic jams that choke modern streets. A single bus can replace dozens of private cars, which means cleaner air and a smaller (5)____ on the environment. Sadly, public transport is often the first service to suffer when budgets are (6)____. Fares rise, routes are cut, and the people who depend on it most are left (7)____. This is short-sighted. Money spent on good transport is not a cost but an (8)____ in the whole community. Cities that build excellent networks find that businesses (9)____, neighbourhoods come alive, and citizens spend less of their lives stuck in traffic. Some of the world's happiest cities are not the wealthiest but those where almost anyone can reach a station within a short walk. There a student, a nurse, and a company director may sit in the very same carriage, sharing the ordinary journey of the day. A well-run bus or train line is, in the end, one of the most democratic things that a modern city can ever offer, because it serves rich and poor (10)____.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 transport,1 convenience,2 active,3 ease,4 burden,5 tight,6 stranded,7 investment,8 thrive,9 alike,10 ignore,11 ancient,12 rapidly,13 purchase,14 lonely。(1) quietly transport millions 运送(动词原形，主语 Buses/trains 复数)=0；(2) beyond simple convenience 便利(名词)=1；(3) remain active in city life 活跃的(形容词作表语)=2；(4) help to ease the traffic jams 缓解(动词原形)=3；(5) a smaller burden on the environment 负担(名词，burden on)=4；(6) when budgets are tight 紧张的(形容词作表语)=5；(7) are left stranded 被困/滞留(过去分词作宾补)=6；(8) an investment in the community 投资(名词)=7；(9) businesses thrive 繁荣(动词原形，主语复数)=8；(10) serves rich and poor alike 同样地(副词，…alike 固定后置)=9。干扰词: ignore(忽视，不合), ancient(古代的，不合), rapidly(副词，但空9为 alike 后置惯用，rapidly 无合适槽), purchase(购买，不合), lonely(孤独的，空7需 stranded 滞留义)。

sets.passage: There was a time when most people knew the names of everyone on their street. Children played outside until dark, and a neighbour was someone you could turn to in a crisis. In many modern cities that sense of belonging has begun to (1)____. People rush home, close their doors, and rarely speak to the family next door. This loss matters more than we often (2)____. A strong neighbourhood is not just a pleasant extra; it is a quiet form of (3)____ against loneliness and fear. When neighbours know one another, they watch out for each other's children, keep an eye on empty houses, and offer help when someone falls (4)____. The good news is that a sense of community can be rebuilt, and it does not require grand schemes. Small, regular acts are often enough to (5)____ trust. A shared garden, a street party, or even a simple habit of greeting people by name can slowly (6)____ the ice. The key is to create natural chances for residents to (7)____. Once people have met a few times, a street stops being a row of strangers and starts to feel like a real (8)____. In some cities, residents have started shared tool libraries, weekend repair cafes, and noticeboards where people offer spare furniture or a few hours of help. None of these ideas is expensive, yet each gives strangers a gentle reason to talk. Belonging, it seems, is not something we are simply (9)____; it is something we build together, slowly, one small kindness at a time. And the rewards of that patient effort are, in the end, well worth the (10)____.
sets.explain_zh: bank 索引: 0 fade,1 realise,2 protection,3 ill,4 build,5 break,6 mingle,7 home,8 given,9 trouble,10 ignore,11 ancient,12 rapidly,13 purchase,14 lonely。(1) belonging has begun to fade 消退(动词原形)=0；(2) more than we realise 意识到(动词原形)=1；(3) a quiet form of protection 保护(名词)=2；(4) someone falls ill 生病(fall ill 固定搭配，形容词)=3；(5) enough to build trust 建立(动词原形)=4；(6) break the ice (break the ice 固定搭配 打破隔阂)=5；(7) chances for residents to mingle 交流/往来(动词原形)=6；(8) feel like a real home 家/归属(名词)=7；(9) something we are simply given 被给予(被动过去分词)=8；(10) worth the trouble 麻烦/付出(名词，worth the trouble)=9。干扰词: ignore(忽视，不合), ancient(古代的，不合), rapidly(副词无槽), purchase(购买，不合), lonely(孤独的，文中已有 loneliness，且各空无形容词'孤独'槽)。
